mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5 percent, it signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 during structural labor shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually, or the interaction term Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and short-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during stable policy environments.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during periods of rapid FEDFUNDS changes, or 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of accelerating INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the series exhibits zero predictive IC over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of months where HOUST drops below 1300, or the threshold effect vanishes when FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal predicts negative returns when inflation exceeds 4%, or the interaction term out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.005 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter when the economy operates near full capacity.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL growth accelerates during periods of slack labor markets, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance with Rank IC below 0 in low-inflation regimes.
